rationale: The "relaxing game" hook and casual female 25-55 audience align very well with Marble Sort's positioning, so the voiceover script and emotional pitch are reusable almost verbatim. However, the visual reference is a bus jam / parking sort game with a bold red-white-blue palette and stylus interaction — that's a different sub-genre and clashes with our pastel lavender + orange physics-tap marble loop, so the centroid b-roll cannot be lifted directly. Recommend adapting the hook script and "hand + question" framing onto native marble-sort footage rather than reusing the bus-clear visuals; otherwise CTR may convert installs into churned users expecting a parking puzzle.
